2606.09663.title: From 0-to-1 to 1-to-N: Reproducible Engineering Evidence for MetaAI Recursive Self-Design
2606.09663.paper_summary: Recursive self-design refers to AI-assisted modification of the mechanisms by which an AI system is built, evaluated, and improved. This paper treats MetaAI not as a mature paradigm, but as a working term for a human-seeded, AI-expanded development pattern in which the design space itself becomes a target of modification. We propose an operational evidence framework with four criteria: inspectable target system, meta-level modifier, feedback-directed selection, and recursive continuation. We then map public systems, including Darwin Goedel Machine (DGM), STOP, Goedel Agent, and ShinkaEvolve, against these criteria. DGM provides the most direct currently reported evidence: its published results show improvement from 20% to 50% on SWE-bench Verified and from 14.2% to 30.7% on full Polyglot after 80 iterations, with ablations suggesting that both open-ended exploration and self-improvement contribute. Finally, we provide MetaAI-Mini, a reproducible HumanEval-based protocol and codebase. Because no completed model run is included in this build, MetaAI-Mini is reported as a protocol rather than as an experimental result.
2606.09663.paper_summary_zh: 遞歸自我設計是指通過人工智慧輔助修改人工智慧系統的構建、評估和改進機制。本文將MetaAI視為一個工作術語，而非成熟範式，這是一種由人類啟發、人工智慧擴展的發展模式，其中設計空間本身成為修改的目標。我們提出了一個操作性證據框架，包含四個標準：可檢查的目標系統、元級修改器、反饋導向的選擇和遞歸延續。接著，我們將公共系統映射到這些標準上，包括達爾文哥德爾機器（DGM）、STOP、哥德爾代理和ShinkaEvolve。DGM提供了目前報告的最直接證據：其發表的結果顯示，在經過80次迭代後，SWE-bench Verified的改進幅度從20%提升至50%，而全Polyglot的改進幅度從14.2%提升至30.7%，消融實驗表明，開放式探索和自我改進均有貢獻。最後，我們提供了MetaAI-Mini，一個可重現的基於HumanEval的協議和代碼庫。由於此構建中不包含任何完整的模型運行，MetaAI-Mini被報告為一個協議，而非實驗結果。
2606.09663.author: Dun Li et.al.
2606.09663.authors: Dun Li, Jiatao Li, Hongzhi Li
